Title: Intra-strain Elicitation and Suppression of Plant Immunity by Ralstonia solanacearum Type-III Effectors in Nicotiana benthamiana

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: HopX/AvrPphB type III effector family; putative catalytic triad-containing effector; conserved domain A
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Plant cell intracellular effector (exact compartment NA)
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Type III-secreted effector encoded by ripE1 in strain GMI1000
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves; estradiol-induced in Arabidopsis seedlings/plants
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Triggers immunity-associated cell death/HR and promotes salicylic acid, jasmonic acid, and glutathione accumulation; enhances resistance to R. solanacearum
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression in N. benthamiana; catalytic mutant C172A and domain A deletion analysis; ion leakage; qRT-PCR of defense/metabolic marker genes; SA/JA/glutathione quantification; pathogen growth assays; inducible Arabidopsis transgenics
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RipE1 from R. solanacearum GMI1000 triggers SGT1-dependent immunity in N. benthamiana and defense activation in Arabidopsis. 2. RipE1 increases SA accumulation in N. benthamiana and Arabidopsis, but correlates with PAL upregulation in N. benthamiana and ICS1 upregulation in Arabidopsis. 3. RipE1 also promotes JA-associated gene expression and JA accumulation, and induces glutathione accumulation before immune activation.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Catalytic cysteine C172 and conserved domain A are required for activity; EDS1 and NRCs are not required for RipE1-triggered cell death in N. benthamiana
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Agrobacterium-mediated expression; estradiol induction in Arabidopsis
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: SGT1-dependent recognition genetically inferred; specific direct host target not identified
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: In Arabidopsis inducible expression reduces plant growth; in N. benthamiana causes HR/cell death
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential avirulence determinant/effector tool for identifying resistance mechanisms, but direct deployment limited because suppression by other effectors can mask recognition
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: SA and JA mediate plant immune responses; glutathione contributes to redox regulation and immunity

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Type III effector; gamma-glutamyl cyclotransferase-related glutathione-degrading effector
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Plant cell intracellular effector (exact compartment NA)
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Type III-secreted effector encoded by ripAY in strain GMI1000
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Suppresses RipE1-triggered immune responses by degrading cellular glutathione, thereby reducing SA-related defense outputs
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression with RipE1 in N. benthamiana; ion leakage; qRT-PCR of NbPR1 and other SA-related genes; comparison with catalytic mutant RipAYE216Q
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RipAY suppresses RipE1-triggered tissue collapse and ion leakage in N. benthamiana. 2. RipAY suppresses RipE1-induced NbPR1 and other SA-related gene expression. 3. Suppression requires RipAY glutathione-degrading activity because RipAYE216Q is inactive.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Did not significantly suppress NbLOX2 or NbAOS expression, suggesting stronger impact on SA-related branch than JA-related branch
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Plant cytosolic thioredoxins reported in prior work for activation; not directly tested here
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Suppressing immunity benefits pathogen virulence; no plant breeding value as positive trait
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for disease-resistance engineering by blocking its glutathione-degrading activity or recognition of its activity
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Glutathione supports redox homeostasis and immune responses

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Endogenous immune regulator; silenced by VIGS
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for RipE1-triggered immunity and associated SA marker gene induction
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: VIGS silencing followed by RipE1 expression, ion leakage, and NbPR1 qRT-PCR
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NbSGT1 is required for RipE1-triggered cell death. 2. NbSGT1 is required for RipE1-triggered ion leakage. 3. NbSGT1 is required for RipE1-triggered NbPR1 induction, linking it to immunity-associated SA signaling.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: EDS1 and NRCs were not required, narrowing the recognition pathway
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Immune signaling component rather than direct metabolic engineering target
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: SA is a central hormone in plant disease resistance

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Deaminates phenylalanine to trans-cinnamic acid, initiating the PAL branch linked to salicylic acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies precursor pool for salicylic acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established in this paper; treated as pathway-associated marker/supporting step
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant immune signaling and disease resistance
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated upon RipE1 expression but not catalytic mutant
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: RipE1 induces NbPAL05 expression, supporting PAL-mediated SA biosynthesis in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR after RipE1 versus RipE1 C172A expression; SA quantification
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NbPAL05 transcripts increase upon expression of catalytically active RipE1. 2. This increase correlates with SA accumulation while NbICS1 decreases. 3. Data support the PAL pathway contributing to RipE1-triggered SA production in N. benthamiana.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to strengthen SA-associated immunity, though direct causal editing was not tested
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Inferred as part of unconventional PAL-mediated SA pathway highlighted by authors

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Deaminates phenylalanine to trans-cinnamic acid, initiating the PAL branch linked to salicylic acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies precursor pool for salicylic acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant immune signaling and disease resistance
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated upon RipE1 expression but not catalytic mutant
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: RipE1 induces NbPAL08 expression, supporting PAL-mediated SA biosynthesis in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR after RipE1 versus RipE1 C172A expression; SA quantification
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NbPAL08 is induced by active RipE1. 2. This induction accompanies SA accumulation and defense activation. 3. The data support participation of the PAL route in RipE1-associated SA biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential immunity-enhancing candidate; direct engineering not tested
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as one of several PAL genes supporting pathway inference

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Deaminates phenylalanine to trans-cinnamic acid, initiating the PAL branch linked to salicylic acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies precursor pool for salicylic acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant immune signaling and disease resistance
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated upon RipE1 expression but not catalytic mutant
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: RipE1 induces NbPAL10 expression, supporting PAL-mediated SA biosynthesis in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR after RipE1 versus RipE1 C172A expression; SA quantification
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NbPAL10 is induced by RipE1. 2. This induction occurs together with increased SA accumulation. 3. Authors infer involvement of PAL-mediated SA biosynthesis in N. benthamiana.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to reinforce defense-associated SA production; not functionally tested alone
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Part of multiple PAL loci induced by RipE1

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts chorismate to isochorismate in the chloroplast, initiating the major pathogen-induced SA biosynthetic route in Arabidopsis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate pathway intermediate for salicylic acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Predominant/key SA biosynthetic route in Arabidopsis as discussed by authors
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant immune signaling and disease resistance
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced upon RipE1 expression in Arabidopsis seedlings
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: RipE1 induces AtICS1 expression, consistent with ICS-mediated SA signaling activation in Arabidopsis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Estradiol-inducible RipE1 expression in transgenic lines; qRT-PCR of AtICS1 and AtPR1
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtICS1 expression increases upon RipE1 induction in Arabidopsis. 2. This correlates with increased AtPR1 expression and enhanced resistance to R. solanacearum. 3. Unlike in N. benthamiana, Arabidopsis relies on the ICS branch rather than PAL1 expression in this context.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful SA-pathway target for strengthening Arabidopsis-like immune responses, though no direct engineering tested here
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Associated immune activation coincides with growth reduction in RipE1-expressing plants
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Contrasts with NbICS1 suppression in N. benthamiana, emphasizing species-specific SA pathway usage

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxygenates polyunsaturated fatty acids to generate hydroperoxy fatty acid intermediates for jasmonate biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies precursor pool for jasmonic acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant defense signaling, especially stress and pathogen responses
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: RipE1 induces NbLOX2 expression in parallel with increased JA accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NbLOX2 transcripts increase upon active RipE1 expression. 2. This correlates with elevated JA levels, indicating activation of JA biosynthesis. 3. RipAY does not significantly suppress this induction.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential lever for defense-hormone modulation, but simultaneous SA-JA activation may create pathogen-specific outcomes
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: JA activation may not enhance resistance to all pathogens and can have antagonistic/complex effects
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Highlights cooperative SA-JA response in this system

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts fatty acid hydroperoxides into unstable allene oxide intermediates in jasmonate biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate pathway intermediate for jasmonic acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: RipE1 induces NbAOS expression together with enhanced JA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NbAOS is induced by active RipE1. 2. This induction accompanies increased JA accumulation. 3. RipAY does not significantly suppress this JA-associated gene induction.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential component for manipulating JA-mediated defense outputs, with pathogen-context caveats
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: JA activation can have context-dependent effects on susceptibility/resistance
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Part of the unexpectedly induced JA branch despite strong SA signaling
